_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron)
_meta.note: Mixed LOCAL batch (5) — VA Chesapeake Mayor West (R, sitting since 2017, re-elected Nov 2024) + GA Atlanta Mayor Dickens (D, sitting 61st mayor, re-elected Nov 2025, 2nd term sworn Jan 5 2026; STATE CORRECTION FL→GA) + FL Cocoa Council D2 Deputy Mayor Hearn (D, sitting since 2020) + FL Cocoa Council D4 Koss (D, sitting since 2018, re-elected Nov 2022) + FL Melbourne Council D1 Smith (nonpartisan, sitting since Nov 2024, term to Nov 2028). Rosters web-verified June 2026 against city sites. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a primary or reputable source; null where no evidence (no party-default inference). Dickens cells limited to documented contrary positions per rubric definitions; refuse_state_overreach q0/q4 left null where Dickens' actions don't fit the rubric's UNCONSTITUTIONAL-directive frame. Public_justice q0 held to the AFFIRMATIVE advocacy bar (Dickens TRUE via Cop City advocacy; West TRUE via pro-police statements; Hearn/Koss/Smith NULL — first-responder career or NNO committee service does not qualify alone).

set.office: Mayor of Chesapeake, Virginia (sitting since Nov 14, 2017 · re-elected Nov 5, 2024 · current term ends Jan 1, 2029)

profile.religion: No public faith profession located (no denomination/church reference on his official, campaign, Wikipedia, Ballotpedia, or local-press footprint as of June 2026)
profile.background: Dr. Richard W. 'Rick' West — born/raised in rural Chesapeake (Great Bridge); father U.S. Navy. B.S. Old Dominion University 1974, M.Ed. Univ. of Virginia 1979, Ed.D. Virginia Tech 1998. 20+ years public-school administrator (taught government and history). Chesapeake City Council 2008–2017; Mayor since Nov 14, 2017; re-elected Nov 5, 2024 (term to Jan 2029). Hampton Roads Alliance board member. Married to Vickie West.

rick-west.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Mayor of Chesapeake as of June 2026 (re-elected Nov 5, 2024; delivered State of the City Apr 9, 2026). Profile reads as a low-ideology, regionalism/development-focused R mayor with little public culture-war record. HARD evidence cited: (a) economic_stewardship q2 TRUE — Apr 2025 publicly withdrew a proposed real estate tax-rate increase after community opposition, choosing fiscal restraint over the hike; (b) public_justice q0 TRUE — affirmative pro-police statements in State-of-the-City and after Holly Cove triple shooting; (c) refuse_state_overreach q0 TRUE + self_defense q1 TRUE — Dec 2019 presided over unanimous 2A 'Constitutional City' resolution explicitly aimed at Virginia Dems' then-pending red-flag / mag-limit / AWB package, with on-record statement defending 'the rights granted to us by both the United States Constitution and the Constitution of Virginia'; (d) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — personally issued Chesapeake's first-ever Pride Month proclamation June 2024 and acknowledged it at council (DEVIATION FLAG: atypical for an R mayor, this is the standout left-side signal). No public faith profession located — unusual for a Bible-belt-suburb R mayor. All other 25 cells null absent direct statement/vote.

4.note: Personally issued Chesapeake's first-ever Pride Month proclamation June 2024, stating LGBTQIA+ individuals have had 'immeasurable impact to the cultural, civic and economic successes of our community' — affirmative state promotion of LGBTQ+ identity

2.note: Apr 2025 publicly withdrew proposed real estate tax-rate increase after community opposition — chose fiscal restraint over revenue hike

0.note: Affirmative pro-police statements — publicly thanked Chesapeake police/first responders; pledged continued work with Chesapeake police on neighborhood-violence prevention after Holly Cove triple shooting

Dec 2019 presided over unanimous Chesapeake 2A 'Constitutional City' resolution; on-record statement: 'I believe you will leave here tonight with confidence that your council will do everything within our powers to preserve the rights granted to us by both the United States Constitution and the Constitution of Virginia' — explicit assertion of local-constitutional posture vs Virginia state gun mandates

1.note: Dec 2019 2A 'Constitutional City' resolution he supported was expressly aimed at Virginia Democrats' then-pending red-flag, magazine-limit, and assault-weapons-ban package

set.office: Mayor of Atlanta, Georgia (sitting · 61st Mayor · re-elected Nov 4, 2025 with ~80%+ · 2nd term sworn Jan 5, 2026 · term ends Jan 2030)

profile.religion: Christian — Baptist; deacon at New Horizon Baptist Church Atlanta (~20 years) and teaches Sunday school weekly; pastor Rev. Sean Smith is his Senior Adviser
profile.background: Andre Dickens, b. June 17, 1974 in Atlanta (Adamsville/Westside). BS Chemical Engineering, Georgia Tech (1998); MPA, Georgia State University (2013). Career: engineer; Asst. Director Office of Institute Diversity at Georgia Tech; chief development officer at a tech nonprofit. Atlanta City Council at-large 2013–2022 (chaired Public Safety & Legal Administration Committee); elected 61st Mayor of Atlanta in 2021 runoff (defeated Felicia Moore); re-elected Nov 4, 2025 with 80%+ (AP called race within 20 minutes of polls closing). Sworn in for 2nd term Jan 5, 2026, at Georgia State University Convocation Center; term ends January 2030. Single; daughter Bailey Rain Dickens. Left X/Twitter for Bluesky.

andre-dickens.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] DATA-FIX: state corrected FL→GA (Atlanta is in Georgia, not Florida — prior record had wrong state). Web-verified sitting Mayor of Atlanta as of June 2026; 2nd term sworn Jan 5, 2026. Conventional Democratic urban-mayor profile on culture-war cells, BUT with three documented atypical-for-D deviation flags: (a) FAMOUS pro-police affirmative advocacy via Atlanta Public Safety Training Center ('Cop City') — pushed through years of national protest, plans to add 400 APD officers — public_justice q0 TRUE; (b) AGGRESSIVE fiscal discipline — erased ~$33M deficit via 5–10% departmental cuts and ~150 FTE reductions, FY26 unanimous balanced budget, FY27 $994.7M balanced proposal, AAA Fitch rating affirmed — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE; (c) 20-year Baptist deacon who teaches Sunday school weekly — christian_liberty q0 TRUE (genuine, long-tenure public Christian profession atypical for a deep-blue mayor to foreground). Documented FALSE positions (cited): life q0/q1 ('sickened by Dobbs', deprioritized GA anti-abortion enforcement, city Reproductive Health & Wellness Rights page); marriage q0 ('LGBTQ capital of the South…regardless of who you love'), q2 ($55K→$105K transgender-community funding incl. gender-expansive youth peer training), q4 (7th annual Pride Reception at City Hall, HRC Municipal Equality Index 100%+16 bonus, LGBTQ Cultural Center feasibility study); family q1 (funded gender-expansive youth peer training without parental-consent emphasis); election q1 (championed John Lewis VRA, framed GA SB202 as voter suppression); public_justice q1 (city ordinance eliminated cash bonds for ordinance violations; affirmatively endorsed Fulton DA Fani Willis), q3 ('It's the guns' public-health open letter framing); refuse_state_overreach q2 (day-one extended COVID indoor mask mandate Jan 2022); border q1 (hopes for 'small or nonexistent' ICE presence at 2026 World Cup) + q2 (Atlanta on non-cooperating-cities list per CIS; council resolutions monitor ICE activity and oppose detention center); self_defense q0 (calls for universal background checks) + q1 (explicit call to ban AWB, mag limits, mandate safe storage, enact red-flag laws). Refuse_state_overreach q0/q4 left null where his actions don't fit the rubric's 'UNCONSTITUTIONAL state directive' frame (his refusal to enforce GA anti-abortion law is a refusal of CONSTITUTIONAL state authority, separate from the rubric question).

0.note: 'I am sickened by this decision' on Dobbs; 'government should not have a role in denying that choice' — contrary to affirming personhood from conception

1.note: Publicly backed City Council resolution de-prioritizing enforcement of GA anti-abortion laws; city stood up Reproductive Health & Wellness Rights & Resources page

0.note: Official statement: 'We proudly stand as the LGBTQ capital of the South…regardless of who you are or who you love' — contradicts one-man-one-woman framing

2.note: Allocated $55K then $105K explicitly to 'Atlanta's transgender community' incl. transgender/gender-expansive youth peer training; explored gender-affirming care for City employees and dependents despite GA youth ban

4.note: Hosts annual Pride Reception at City Hall (7th annual 2025); LGBTQ+ Affairs Biennial Report; LGBTQ Cultural/Educational/Recreational Center feasibility study; HRC Municipal Equality Index 100%+16 bonus

1.note: Funded gender-expansive youth peer-support training without parental-consent emphasis; framed as protecting trans youth against state restrictions

0.note: Long-tenure (~20 years) deacon at New Horizon Baptist Church Atlanta; teaches Sunday school weekly; pastor Rev. Sean Smith is his Senior Adviser; 2026 inauguration week included inter-faith service; spoke at Candler School of Theology convocation — sustained public profession of Christ

2.note: Reduced ~$33M deficit via 5–10% departmental cuts and ~150 FTE reductions; FY2026 unanimous balanced budget; FY2027 $994.7M balanced proposal; Fitch affirmed AAA rating; admin language explicit on 'fiscally responsible' / 'balanced budget' / 'does not sell off City assets'

1.note: Public alignment with Abrams-coalition framing of GA SB202 as voter suppression; campaigned to 'fight to support the John Lewis Voting Rights Act' — contrary to photo-ID/citizenship-verification posture

0.note: Famously the public-face backer of the Atlanta Public Safety Training Center ('Cop City') through years of legal battles, protests, and violence; plan to add 400 APD officers in two years; 'One Safe City' plan — affirmative pro-police advocacy (clears the high-bar discipline)

1.note: City ordinance eliminates cash bonds for city-ordinance violations (justicereform.atlantaga.gov 'Cash Bond Reform'); publicly endorsed and defended Fulton DA Fani Willis

3.note: Open letter 'It's the guns' frames violence through public-health/gun-access lens — explicit adoption of the violent-crime-as-public-health framing the rubric rejects

2.note: Day-one as mayor Jan 2022 signed executive orders EXTENDING COVID indoor mask mandates in public spaces and private businesses — imposed lockdown-era overreach rather than protecting citizens from it

1.note: Publicly stated he hopes ICE presence at 2026 World Cup is 'small or nonexistent'; pushed back on federal presence at Hartsfield-Jackson — contrary to mandatory-deportation posture

2.note: Atlanta listed among non-cooperating cities; council passed resolutions monitoring ICE activity and opposing detention center construction with 'commitment to immigrant justice' framing — affirmative sanctuary-leaning posture

0.note: Calls for universal background checks — contrary to constitutional-carry posture

1.note: Explicit call to ban assault weapons and high-capacity magazines, mandate safe storage, expand universal background checks, and enact red-flag laws

set.office: City Council, District 2 (Deputy Mayor), City of Cocoa, Florida (sitting since Nov 2020 · Deputy Mayor since Nov 2021)

profile.background: Lavander Hearn — Cocoa High School alum; Eastern Florida State College + University of Florida; AS Fire Science, BBA, MS Emergency Management/Disaster Mitigation. Brevard County Fire Rescue Firefighter/EMT; substitute teacher Brevard Public Schools; currently works in Emergency Management field. As a teen had 'incidents involving the law' and was turned around via youth mentorship — informs his youth-outreach focus. Cocoa City Council District 2 since Nov 2020; selected Deputy Mayor unanimously by Council Nov 2021. Appointed to Florida League of Cities Young Elected Municipal Officials Advisory Council. Brevard County Democratic Party rostered.

lavander-hearn.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 2 Councilman / Deputy Mayor of Cocoa as of June 2026 (cocoafl.gov bio live; 2024 reelection cycle timing consistent with continuing term through ~2028). Confidence held at roster_verified — exhaustive 10-min sweep of cocoafl.gov, Cocoa Council minutes, Florida Today, ClickOrlando, Spectrum News 13, Space Coast Daily, Brevard County Democratic Party site, and his official Facebook page surfaced ONE documented policy action (May 2022 rent-notice ordinance, a tenant-protection measure that doesn't map to any of the 30 LOCAL-rubric cells) and no statements on any culture-war / fiscal / criminal-justice / immigration / 2A / election cell. All 30 cells null — honest, not lazy. First-responder career is first-responder-allied but, per skill discipline, does NOT qualify as affirmative public 'back the blue' advocacy for public_justice q0. Documented policy footprint: rent-notice ordinance only.

set.office: City Council, District 4, City of Cocoa, Florida (sitting since 2018 · re-elected Nov 2022 · term through Nov 2026)

profile.background: Lorraine Koss — Retired Deputy Director, U.S. Water Alliance. Master's in Environmental Management, Duke University; Bachelor's in Communications, Trinity University. 30+ years water-resources policy. Founded Cocoa Sustainability Advisory Committee; chaired Utility Advisory Board; first chair and continuing member of the Save Our Indian River Lagoon Citizen Oversight Committee (steering $300M, 10-year lagoon plan); organized FL SUN Space Coast Solar Co-op. Serves on Space Coast EDC and Space Coast Transportation Planning Organization Executive Committee. Appointed to Florida League of Cities 2024-2025 Utilities & Natural Resources Committee. Cocoa City Council District 4 since 2018; re-elected November 2022. Married to Lawrence Koss; two sons (each served five tours in Iraq); one grandson. Self-describes in 2022 as 'an independent voice'; listed as Democratic elected official on BrevardDems site.

lorraine-koss.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 4 Councilwoman of Cocoa as of June 2026 (cocoafl.gov bio + Wikipedia city article confirm). Profile is a sustainability/water/stormwater specialist — heavy regional-board entanglement (FL League of Cities, EDC, TPO, Save Our Indian River Lagoon committee). HARD evidence cited: self_defense q1 FALSE — per profile, 'worked with Ohioans for Gun Safety on a ballot initiative for universal background checks on gun sales' — documented pre-Cocoa-Council active support for expanded gun-control via background-check expansion, contrary to rubric's opposition to registries/universal-check mandates. All other 29 cells null after sweep of cocoafl.gov, Council minutes, Florida Today, ClickOrlando, Spectrum News 13, Space Coast Daily, BrevardDems site, LinkedIn (fetch-blocked), and campaign Facebook. No public faith profession, no abortion/marriage/sexuality/school-policy/immigration/police statements located.

1.note: Profile documents pre-Cocoa-Council active support for expanded gun control: 'worked with Ohioans for Gun Safety on a ballot initiative for universal background checks on gun sales' — contrary to rubric's opposition to registries / universal-check mandates

set.office: City Council, District 1, City of Melbourne, Florida (sitting since Nov 2024 · term to Nov 2028)

profile.background: Marcus Smith — Retired U.S. Air Force Lt. Col., ~30 yrs combined service (Navy enlisted → AF officer, retired 2021). B.S. Electrical Engineering Georgia Tech; MBA Webster University. Financial representative at Sky Peak Financial; owner Tax Time Solutions LLC since 2005. Chaired Melbourne Planning & Zoning 2021–2024. American Legion Post 191 1st Vice. Kappa Alpha Psi alumnus; Mason (Lodge #318). First elected Nov 5, 2024 with ~52% in nonpartisan general; term to Nov 2028. Serves on Melbourne National Night Out planning committee since 2020. Endorsed by Krimson PAC.

marcus-smith.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 1 Council Member of Melbourne as of June 2026 (term to Nov 2028 per melbourneflorida.org). Nonpartisan seat but documented voting record reads as center-left independent — NOT the assumed 'retired military = likely conservative' prior. HARD evidence cited: economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — Sept 23/24, 2025 voted AYE to adopt $315.4M FY2025-26 budget and set operating millage at 7.0112 mills, an 11.4% INCREASE over the rollback rate (functionally a tax hike on residents). All other 29 cells null after sweep of melbourneflorida.org Council minutes, Florida Today, Space Coast Daily, Fox 35 Orlando, Spectrum News 13, FL Voice News, his campaign site, and Brevard GOP/Dem sites. DEVIATION FLAGS (not cell-scored): (a) Sept 24, 2025 voted NO on Mayor Alfrey's proposal to rename Cypress Ave 'Charlie Kirk Lane' after Kirk's assassination, with stated rationale 'many of his statements were divisive' — per prior Melbourne-batch Kirk Lane discipline this leaves christian_liberty NULL because the rationale was 'divisive' not explicitly anti-Christian; (b) endorsed by Krimson PAC (self-described progressive PAC). National Night Out planning-committee service is community engagement, NOT affirmative public 'back the blue' advocacy — public_justice q0 NULL per skill discipline.

2.note: Sept 23/24, 2025 voted AYE to adopt $315.4M FY2025-26 budget and set operating millage at 7.0112 mills, an 11.4% INCREASE over the rollback rate — functionally a tax increase on residents, contrary to fiscal-restraint posture
